channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA prepares to launch daring rescue to save aging telescope from falling to Earth - CBS News
item.description:
1. NASA prepares to launch daring rescue to save aging telescope from falling to Earth  CBS News
  2. Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Inside NASA’s daring race to save a doomed space telescope  USA Today
  4. NASA is paying $30 million for a 1st-of-its-kind rescue mission to the aging Swift telescope before it falls from space. Is it worth it?  Space
  5. This Week, NASA Will Launch a First-of-Its-Kind Mission to Save a Space Telescope That Has Observed the Sky for Nearly 22 Years  Smithsonian Magazine

item.title: Scientists Think They’ve Uncovered the 15-Million-Year-Old Origin of Laughter - 404 Media
item.description:
1. Scientists Think They’ve Uncovered the 15-Million-Year-Old Origin of Laughter  404 Media

  4. To Reveal the Rhythmic Roots of Laughter, Just Tickle an Ape  The New York Times
  5. What an ape’s laugh can teach us about human language  National Geographic

item.title: In 2020, something near the centre of the Milky Way switched on and off six times in radio waves, then disappeared from every follow-up search in X-rays and infrared — leaving astronomers with only the name ASKAP J173608.2−321635 and the possibility of - Space Daily

item.title: Complex life may have started before Earth had much oxygen at all. A University of Bristol-led Nature study found that the ancient archaeal lineage behind all plants, animals and fungi began developing complex cellular machinery in anoxic oceans almost 2.9 - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Complex life may have started before Earth had much oxygen at all. A University of Bristol-led Nature study found that the ancient archaeal lineage behind all plants, animals and fungi began developing complex cellular machinery in anoxic oceans almost 2.9  Space Daily
  2. Living rocks hidden in Australia's salty waters intrigue scientists by revealing how microbes helped transform Earth's atmosphere 2.4 billion years ago.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.description:
1. Black hole's 'point of no escape' studied with the loudest gravitational waves ever heard  Space

  4. A New Way to Touch the Most Violent Gravity in the Universe  ScienceBlog.com
  5. Whirlpool in Spacetime Around a Black Hole Merger Provides First Detection of this General Relativity Prediction  The Debrief

item.title: Science news this week: Life on Mars, weird water and a curious human cousin - Live Science
item.description:
1. Science news this week: Life on Mars, weird water and a curious human cousin  Live Science
  2. A Mars rover found organic carbon just sitting on a rock  Science News
  3. In an ancient Martian river channel, Perseverance drilled into a mudstone called Cheyava Falls and found tiny “leopard spot” minerals tangled with organic carbon — patterns that, on Earth, can be left behind when microbes consume organic matter, though sci  Space Daily
  4. Nasa rover detects potential signatures of ancient microbial life on Mars  The Guardian
  5. NASA Rover Finds "Complex Organic Matter" on Mars  Futurism

item.title: China was effectively shut out of the International Space Station after the United States passed the Wolf Amendment in 2011, so it built its own. If the ISS retires on schedule around 2030 and no commercial successor is ready, China's Tiangong could become t - Space Daily
item.description:
1. China was effectively shut out of the International Space Station after the United States passed the Wolf Amendment in 2011, so it built its own. If the ISS retires on schedule around 2030 and no commercial successor is ready, China's Tiangong could become t  Space Daily
  2. China plans to double the size of its Tiangong space station while the ISS nears its end  Space
  3. China’s Space Station Set to Expand  Inside Outer Space
  4. China plans to expand Tiangong space station to boost scientific capacity (with video)  Plataforma Media
  5. China's space station to be expanded to unlock broader future_InKunming  昆明信息港

  5. A Blue Origin rocket explosion leaves NASA leaning harder on SpaceX, and the Moon race suddenly has fewer engines to trust  Vozpopuli

item.title: Astronomers used to assume galaxies formed first and slowly grew the black holes at their centres. But JWST is now finding objects in the early universe where the black hole appears to have arrived first — already enormous before a full galaxy had grown aro - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Astronomers used to assume galaxies formed first and slowly grew the black holes at their centres. But JWST is now finding objects in the early universe where the black hole appears to have arrived first — already enormous before a full galaxy had grown aro  Space Daily
  2. James Webb Captures Strongest Evidence Yet for Black Hole Stars  DIYPhotography
  3. This mysterious “red dot” stuns scientists: a black hole before its galaxy?  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  4. James Webb detects signs of what could be the first black hole stars in the universe  yourweather.co.uk

  4. Scientists find ancient microbial life in places it shouldn't exist  The News International

item.title: Just 18 light-years away, astronomers have found one of the best nearby targets in the search for life: a candidate super-Earth called GJ 251 c, sitting in the habitable zone of a red dwarf star. Future giant telescopes may be able to image it directly — and look f - Space Daily
item.description: Just 18 light-years away, astronomers have found one of the best nearby targets in the search for life: a candidate super-Earth called GJ 251 c, sitting in the habitable zone of a red dwarf star. Future giant telescopes may be able to image it directly — and look f  Space Daily

item.description: Time may be an illusion derived from quantum entanglement  The Brighter Side of News

item.title: In 1977, Ann Druyan recorded an hour of her brainwaves and heartbeat just two days after she and Carl Sagan agreed to marry, and NASA pressed that compressed minute onto Voyager's Golden Record as a private love letter now more than 25 billion kilome - Space Daily
item.description: In 1977, Ann Druyan recorded an hour of her brainwaves and heartbeat just two days after she and Carl Sagan agreed to marry, and NASA pressed that compressed minute onto Voyager's Golden Record as a private love letter now more than 25 billion kilome  Space Daily

item.title: Only one spacecraft has ever visited Uranus or Neptune: Voyager 2, which flew past Uranus in 1986 and Neptune in 1989. Nearly four decades later, no mission has returned, leaving many of the biggest questions those flybys raised — about the planets’ interi - Space Daily
item.description: Only one spacecraft has ever visited Uranus or Neptune: Voyager 2, which flew past Uranus in 1986 and Neptune in 1989. Nearly four decades later, no mission has returned, leaving many of the biggest questions those flybys raised — about the planets’ interi  Space Daily

item.title: A bowhead whale can live for more than 200 years, yet it appears unusually resistant to cancer — and its genome carries genetic changes that may help explain why. Researchers have found signs of enhanced DNA repair in bowhead cells, turning one of the - Space Daily
